channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Brandi Carlile Raises $600,000 for Families Affected by Immigration Enforcement in Minneapolis
item.description: Grammy-winning singer Brandi Carlile transformed her Minneapolis concert into a significant fundraiser, generating over $600,000 to support families impacted by recent Immigration and Customs Enforcem...

item.title: Emerging Chinese Private Rocket Companies: Zenk Space and Others
item.description: Tracking the Chinese private space sector can be challenging due to its rapid growth. Established companies like LandSpace, iSpace, and Galactic Energy have already gained attention, but new players c...

item.title: Dakota Johnson reflects on her past relationship with Chris Martin and moves forward
item.description: Dakota Johnson has reportedly moved on from her on-and-off relationship with Coldplay singer Chris Martin, which lasted for eight years and ended last year. According to sources, Johnson regrets givin...

item.title: Russell Brand Denies New Rape and Sexual Assault Allegations in London Court
item.description: British comedian and actor Russell Brand, 50, appeared at Southwark Crown Court in London on February 24, 2026, to plead not guilty to two new charges of rape and sexual assault. The alleged incidents...

item.title: From Gutenberg’s Printing Press to Generative AI: The Dialectics of Knowledge and the Revival of Orality
item.description: The article reflects on the historical transformation of knowledge transmission, drawing a parallel between the invention of Gutenberg’s movable-type printing press in the 15th century and the current...

item.title: Reports Highlight Sanitation Problems Aboard USS Gerald R. Ford Amid Broader Military Operations
item.description: The article reports on alleged sanitation system failures aboard the USS Gerald R. Ford, the lead ship of its class and one of the most expensive aircraft carriers ever built by the United States Navy...

item.title: Deregulation of Electricity in Ohio Increased Costs Through Middlemen
item.description: Recent research from The Ohio State University’s Energy Markets and Policy Group shows that electricity deregulation in several U.S. states, including Ohio, has led to higher, not lower, prices for co...

item.title: USS Gerald R. Ford Faces Persistent Sewage System Failures During Extended Deployment Amid U.S.-Iran Tensions
item.description: The USS Gerald R. Ford, the U.S. Navy's most advanced and expensive aircraft carrier at $13.3 billion, is grappling with chronic sewage system problems while deployed in the Middle East. The nuclear-p...

item.title: Primal Fathers, Billionaire Households, and the Political Economy of Sexual Power
item.description: This article examines the ideological and economic logic linking Silicon Valley pronatalism, far-right political movements, and the household empire built by Jeffrey Epstein. It begins by describing t...

item.title: Rising Sigilkore Rapper Luci4 Dies at 23 in Los Angeles
item.description: Rapper Luci4, born James Dear and also known by the stage name Axxturel, has died at the age of 23. According to a report from the Los Angeles County Medical Examiner, he passed away on Sunday, Februa...

item.title: Global Report Highlights Concentration of Geopolitical Cyberattacks in Ten Key Countries

item.title: Updated Analysis of Global Industrial and Population Trends
item.description: This article examines recent updates to the World3 model, originally developed in the 1970s to study the interaction between population growth, industrial production, resource consumption, and environ...

item.title: Foreign Patients Increasingly Visit Chinese Hospitals for Affordable and Efficient Medical Care
item.description: In recent years, China has become a popular destination for international medical tourists seeking affordable and fast healthcare. Cities like Shenzhen and Shanghai have seen significant increases in ...

item.title: DOJ Faces Questions After Epstein-Related Record Briefly Disappears From Public Database
item.description: The article examines controversy surrounding the U.S. Department of Justice (DOJ) after a record connected to the Jeffrey Epstein case briefly disappeared from a public database before being restored....

item.title: Practical Strategies for Combining Comfort and Style in Home Design
item.description: This article explores how to harmonize aesthetics and functionality in home design by prioritizing comfort alongside visual appeal. It emphasizes the importance of understanding how people experience ...

item.title: Russia Links Attempted Assassination of General Alexeiev to International Involvement
item.description: On February 6, 2026, an attempt was made on the life of Russian General Vladimir Alexeiev, who was shot multiple times but survived after being hospitalized and undergoing surgery. Russian authorities...

item.title: Investigation Alleges Israeli Forces Shot and Killed 15 Gaza Aid Workers During March 2025 Operation
item.description: A joint investigation by the research groups Earshot and Forensic Architecture alleges that Israeli soldiers killed 15 Palestinian aid workers in Tel al-Sultan, southern Gaza, on March 23, 2025. Accor...

item.title: Artemis II Launch Postponed to April Due to Helium System Issue
item.description: The launch of Artemis II, NASA’s second mission in the Artemis program, has been postponed to at least April 2026 due to an issue with the helium pressurization system on the rocket's ICPS second stag...

item.title: Computational Study Finds 40,000-Year-Old Stone Age Sign Sequences Comparable in Complexity to Proto-Cuneiform
item.description: A new study by linguist Christian Bentz of Saarland University and archaeologist Ewa Dutkiewicz from the Museum of Prehistory and Early History in Berlin suggests that sign sequences carved into Stone...

item.title: Media Manipulation in the Ukraine War: Glenn Diesen at the UN Security Council
item.description: This video features Glenn Diesen, a researcher, presenting at the UN Security Council. He analyzes how media coverage of the Ukraine war may be biased and used to shape public perception, emphasizing ...
